updateRules.title: Product rules and Website Hub sync
updateRules.ownerAgent: AIM Website Product Rules Sync Watch
policy: Do not invent product rules when sources conflict.
If implementation code contradicts the registry, report drift with exact files and lines.
Do not push or deploy.

updateRules.title: Case source fidelity review
updateRules.cadence: before every new or materially updated case study handoff
updateRules.trigger: manual Codex work, case-source update, YouTube fragment extraction, or user-provided case notes
inputs: YouTube fragment URL, transcript, or downloaded source frames
outputs: verified case task, description, tools, result, and media notes
clean source-cropped screenshots and speaker portrait when needed
policy: Do not publish a case with technology claims that are not evidenced in the exact case source set.
For YouTube cases, crop relevant video frames and remove browser, OS, and player UI unless it is part of the case evidence.
Unsupported facts must be removed, marked unverified, or returned to the user as a question.
Do not push or deploy.

updateRules.title: Production/staging QA dashboards
updateRules.cadence: manual; health on demand; biweekly candidate every Monday 07:00 UTC with even ISO-week guard; monthly sweep on day 1 at 08:00 UTC
policy: First screenshot run creates baselines; later runs compare against baselines.
High findings make the run failed.
Generated dashboards are published by GitHub Pages when the workflow runs.

updateRules.title: DeviceCloud preflight
updateRules.cadence: manual; monthly preflight on day 2 at 09:00 UTC
policy: Skip cleanly without provider secrets.
Do not make paid provider sessions part of the main QA gate until the adapter is stable.

updateRules.title: Surikat Telegram intake
updateRules.cadence: Cloud Run webhook, always-on while the service is active; local LaunchAgent is rollback only
updateRules.trigger: Telegram webhook to Cloud Run service aim-surikat-vasily /telegram in GCP project aim-surikat-vasily
inputs: Telegram voice/audio/video-note media via getFile for Gemini/Vertex transcription
outputs: LLM site-audit replies for owner-gated read-only tasks
Durable owner-learned runtime rules, ownerRuntimeInstructions, and chat aliases in GCS
local pending queue when Linear is unavailable
Cloud Run audit log mirrored from /tmp/vasily-state/audit-log.jsonl to GCS
local audit log at Bots/Telegram Bug Intake Bot - Linear/data/audit-log.jsonl when rollback polling is active
policy: Group chat behavior is conservative.
Addressed messages must be classified semantically before action: visual QA/full-pass/audit/check tasks, concrete site breakage, and questions/discussion must not collapse into one generic route.

For addressed questions/discussion/bot-flow critique, LLM may write only the final short reply after hard guards and semantic intent classification; it must not accept tasks, create Linear issues, or invent QA/dashboard status.
Owner tone feedback such as 'говори нормально' or 'не по-ишному' updates conversationalReplyStyle and must not be treated as just another chat question.
Broader owner instructions such as 'запомни правило', 'когда я говорю...', or 'в следующий раз...' update ownerRuntimeInstructions and are injected into future conversational and task prompts.
Telegram text_link entities count as URLs for task recognition.

Telegram voice/audio/video-note attachments must be transcribed through Gemini/Vertex before routing; the transcript counts as effective text for URL extraction, semantic classification, and private owner context memory.
URL-backed aliases learned in a chat/thread, such as ai-native, count as task targets for future owner commands in that same thread.
AI Native visual QA output belongs in AIM Site Agent Evaluation as a separate dashboard page linked from the choose-site selector once the local QA/Codex runner is connected.
Do not run Telegram webhook and local getUpdates polling at the same time.
Vasily production Cloud Run and lead relay live in dedicated GCP project aim-surikat-vasily; Sonya remains in project-f40c3e3c-0fca-49de-96d.
Owner-gated LLM site audits are read-only and must not edit the website or push code.
Related Labs/sprint pages must be checked for stale or ending-soon dates, not only HTTP 200.
Product/payment answers must use the Website Hub registry.

Metrika/form/payment lead DMs are allowed only to explicit owner-controlled recipients; the browser-facing aim-pay-lead-relay must filter SURIKAT_METRIKA_LEAD_DM_RECIPIENTS through SURIKAT_METRIKA_LEAD_DM_ALLOWED_USERNAMES and must not fall back to a generic TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID when the allowlist is configured. Payment lead notification pause/resume may also be controlled by configured managers, currently Anca/@stavenski and Ira/@Irhen_N, through Vasily's durable metrikaLeadDmDeliveryPaused state.

Owner Telegram rule changes, generic owner runtime instructions, conversational style feedback, and chat alias memory must hydrate from and persist to the Vasily GCS state prefix before/after webhook processing.
OwnerRuntimeInstructions do not override Metrika DM recipient allowlists, read-only audit limits, secrets, Linear, or deploy guards.
Owner-only tuning commands are accepted only from Anca/@stavenski.

updateRules.title: Bot operating rules and Website Hub sync
updateRules.cadence: on every meaningful bot runtime/behavior change; local preview auto-sync on /aim-site-hub/
updateRules.trigger: manual Codex work, bot-operating-rules sync script, or local preview request to /aim-site-hub/
policy: Bot runtime changes must be visible in the Hub and in the relevant bot README/runtime docs.

Bot rule or architecture changes must update website-ops/bot-operating-rules.json first, then regenerate the Hub summary through local-preview/sync-bot-operating-rules-summary.mjs or local preview auto-sync.
Each Telegram bot must have one delivery owner at a time: webhook or polling, never both.
Secrets stay in Keychain locally or Secret Manager in Cloud Run, not tracked files.
Read-only audit commands from Telegram must not edit the website or push code.

updateRules.title: Website Hub refresh
updateRules.cadence: on every meaningful operational/UI/doc change; generated summaries also refreshed by local preview auto-sync
updateRules.trigger: manual Codex work, product-rules sync script, tool-registry sync script, bot-operating-rules sync script, or local preview request to /aim-site-hub/
policy: After meaningful hub/UI change, provide desktop and LAN/mobile preview links.
The local preview server auto-syncs generated Product rules, Tool registry, and Bot operating rules sections before serving /aim-site-hub/ unless AIM_PREVIEW_AUTO_SYNC_HUB=false is set.
Validate local links and no horizontal overflow on desktop/mobile.
Do not push without explicit review approval and push authorization.

updateRules.title: Design feedback evaluation dashboard
updateRules.cadence: on every explicit design rating/rejection; automated rating evaluator every 4 hours while active; before new design-generation runs
updateRules.trigger: AIM Page Design Lab site rating, Surikat Sonya Telegram rating, manual Codex work, user design review, or Codex automation aim-design-rating-evaluator
policy: Dashboard has three lanes: main homepage design, guided payment page design, and standalone elements/blocks/popups.
AIM Design Quality Pursuit is active: every generated design direction should plausibly score at least 8/10 and match AI Mindset spirit before handoff.
If the agent's own expected rating is below 8/10, iterate, mark the run as failed exploration, or state the exact gap instead of presenting it as good.
Every explicit numeric user rating must be stored with artifact path, diagnosis, and generator impact.
AIM Page Design Lab cards show the average score out of 10 across all explicit ratings and reveal individual ratings on hover/focus; missing reviewer scores stay blank.
AIM Page Design Lab can write ratings through the local preview endpoint /__aim_design_rating; Anka/Sasha/custom site ratings are included in the visible average and retained as generator evidence.
The automated rating evaluator must periodically read the registry and generator handoff, then update docs/manifests/task ledgers or queue follow-up design work according to the latest ratings.
Ratings from 1-3 out of 10 are rejected directions and become hard avoid rules.
Strong negative feedback without a number is still negative memory and should be tagged strong-negative.

Structure-only wireframes, sitemap diagrams, and information-architecture maps must be hidden from the default AIM Page Design Lab gallery; they are internal planning artifacts, not design candidates.

Near-duplicate review skins must be hidden from the default AIM Page Design Lab gallery and Sonya review. Payment 02-05 / Topographic-style variants are the current negative example: same payment card and canvas shell with mostly background/accent/ambient changes is not a set of different designs.

Every new homepage/payment/element review candidate must have a material delta: composition, hierarchy, interaction/motion mechanic, content model, state behavior, or source-mechanic fidelity. If the same shell is intentionally explored, keep at most two variants and record reviewFocus/designDelta.

Before sending payment design review links, run QA for active-control contrast, black-on-dark text, red CTA/active cards, invented checkout copy, compact input typography, route animation placement, discount behavior, and desktop/mobile overflow.
Payment page work must preserve the functional payment UX and cover four states: choose/pay, bank redirect, payment failed, payment succeeded.
Do not invent final success/failure payment copy before the user provides its source.
Do not commit, push, deploy, or replace production/staging pages.

Homepage and element design links must pass a screenshot readability gate before handoff: readable Russian body copy, proportional body font, high contrast, calm background under text, comfortable line-height, and no dense grid/diagram noise behind paragraphs.
AIM OS is a principles/mechanics reference only; do not copy its low-readability monospaced page style, beige/yellow grid, or system-map density into homepage body copy.

If an artifact is materially redesigned after rating, its manifest must set ratingPolicy.ratingResetAt; older ratings become stale context, Page Design Lab shows recheck until a fresh rating exists, and the evaluator should queue Sonya rereview when ratingPolicy.needsSonyaRereview is true.

updateRules.title: Surikat Sonya design review bot
updateRules.cadence: nightly candidate scan and daytime DM review delivery when review windows are known
updateRules.trigger: local catalog publisher, manual /review command, Cloud Run webhook, or future schedule after night-design-pursuit output
inputs: Telegram DM messages and callback_query ratings from @stavenski and @alex_named
Vertex AI via Google Cloud billing credits when SONYA_LLM_PROVIDER=vertex
Gemini Developer API as local fallback when GEMINI_API_KEY is configured
Cloud Storage bucket for incoming Telegram media when SONYA_GCS_BUCKET is configured
GCS catalog.json curated selection and inventory.json broad review inventory published by the local catalog publisher
outputs: Telegram screenshot review cards with 0-10 inline rating buttons
website-ops/design-generator-feedback-handoff.jsonl with next-design instructions for page generators
Cloud Run webhook service when deployed from Bots/Telegram Design Taste Bot - Sonya/
Cloud Storage media objects under SONYA_GCS_PREFIX/telegram-media/ when configured
Bots/Telegram Bug Intake Bot - Linear/data/sonya-design-handoff.jsonl for bug-like design feedback
review-only GitHub Pages links for full-page design lab artifacts when published
GCS state/catalog surfaces for Cloud Run review cards and durable owner/dispatch/registry/handoff state
policy: Curated and scheduled review sends only candidates with sonyaScore strictly greater than 5/10; manual broad review requests may draw from inventory.json to inspect unrated candidates, but still use review-only artifacts and owner-controlled pacing.
Use the full 0-10 rating scale: 0 is hard reject, 1-3 rejected, 4-6 weak, 7-8 promising, 9-10 strong.
Anka and Sasha are the only owners; Sasha must press /start once before Sonya can DM him.
Store Telegram and Gemini secrets outside tracked files, preferably Keychain.
Cloud credits path must use SONYA_LLM_PROVIDER=vertex with VERTEX_PROJECT_ID inside the credit-linked Google Cloud project; plain GEMINI_API_KEY is only the Gemini Developer API fallback.
Do not run Telegram polling and Cloud Run webhook mode at the same time.

Cloud Run uses the GCS state mirror for owner/dispatch/log/registry/handoff surfaces; if hydrate fails while enabled, the webhook acknowledges without side effects instead of overwriting durable state from stale local files.

Cloud Run reads review cards from GCS catalog.json and inventory.json; the local catalog publisher owns scoring, screenshot capture, stale-lock cleanup, published-only public links, and GCS publication.

Current live health should expose mode=webhook, genaiProvider=vertex, groupChatEnabled=true, groupVideoReplyEnabled=true, autoReviewEnabled=false, requireSendApproval=true, and sashaProactiveDmEnabled=false.

Runtime Veo is configured but disabled and expired. The repo Dockerfile runtime has ffmpeg available, so real Telegram video_note output is possible only if the owner explicitly reopens paid generation and health reports runtimeVeo.ffmpegAvailable=true; buildpack/source deploys without ffmpeg must not enable sendAsVideoNote.
Every numeric rating must be passed to the relevant design generator handoff with target automation ids, rating sentiment, what to keep/change/avoid/promote, and a concrete next-generation brief.

Narrow publication exception: Sonya may commit and push review-only design lab artifacts with sonyaScore > 5 without Anka local review, but may not replace live routes, deploy production/staging replacements, or stage unrelated dirty files.
Full-page review cards should include a GitHub Pages URL for the specific page when available.

Review cards should include Na chto smotret / Chem otlichaetsya when the manifest or GCS catalog includes reviewFocus/designDelta. If a candidate is only a near-duplicate skin, hide it instead of asking for another rating.
Explicit low owner ratings override Sonya cached self-scores: a candidate rated <=5/10 by Anca or Sasha is skipped from the curated catalog even if an older background evaluation scored it higher.
Bug-like feedback about a design/page is written to the Sonya-to-Vasily handoff JSONL instead of creating Linear issues directly.
Candidates with ratingPolicy.needsSonyaRereview should be sent again as fresh review cards even if they already have older ratings; caption the previous rating as stale context after redesign.

updateRules.title: Homepage full design generator
updateRules.cadence: weekly on Monday at 09:40 Europe/Lisbon after initial manual burst
inputs: /Users/viola/All/Yandex.Disk.localized/3 Process/8 Vibe Coding/evalv : Creative Taste Bot/generated-animations/manifest.json when available
policy: This is a review-only homepage design lab; do not replace the live React route.
Every full-page run must cover all 13 blocks from website-ops/homepage-design-brief.json.
Use a light theme and improve readability/user flow when borrowing AIM OS visual language.

Before generation, use website-ops/design-curriculum-reference-pack.md as a preflight for project argumentation, visual codes, information clarity, accessibility, composition, storytelling, UI/product, and brand-system reasoning.
Before generation, use design-feedback-dashboard/data/design-feedback-registry.json as a feedback preflight. Active rejected ratings and strong negative feedback must change the next concept.
Before generation, read the latest relevant Sonya generator handoff events and explicitly respond to their keep/change/avoid/promote instructions.
AIM Design Quality Pursuit target is 8+/10. Self-check against AIM spirit and stored feedback before handoff; if expected score is below 8, iterate or mark the run as failed exploration.
The Stas Leto School lesson files are internal reasoning references only; do not copy lesson text into AIM prototypes.
Anca Projects may be used as an owned mechanics reference for mouse-reactive galaxy/starfield backgrounds.
Use Art of Noise only for mechanics: sound-to-typography logic, variable type behavior, disciplined labels plus immersive large-scale type, density/repetition, and spatial rhythm.
Treat external references as inspiration for mechanics, hierarchy, and structure only; do not copy assets or layouts.

Do not copy visible reference text into AIM prototypes. Slide titles, reference labels, exhibition copy, demo captions, and arbitrary numeric labels must be removed or replaced with source-backed AIM/payment/site copy.
Do not create fake proof: no review blocks without real reviews, no archive/project cards without real links, and no source-thin homepage pretending to be complete.
Run a source-fidelity pass for names, links, CTAs, product labels, and footer/legal/archive references before handoff.
Do not commit, push, deploy, or edit production/staging routes.
Exception: Surikat Sonya may publish only review-only design lab artifacts to GitHub Pages after sonyaScore > 5; this does not authorize live route replacement or unrelated staging.
After the 2026-05-28 rejection, do not repeat over-literal AIM OS / beige-grid / monospaced Russian homepage directions or self-rate them as 8/10.
Homepage body copy must use readable proportional typography; monospace is limited to short labels, tags, codes, and metadata.
Before handoff, check desktop/mobile screenshots for immediate readability and hide failed explorations from the default gallery.

Do not generate many variants of the same page shell with only background/accent/ambient changes. At most two same-shell variants may be kept for comparison, and only if the README/manifest states exactly what changed.

Payment Event Horizon learning: keep the mouse-dependent glow from AIM Payment Gravity, but do not repeat simple vertical galaxy sway. The rejected AIM Event Horizon was a local 2D approximation, not a Gemini-assisted review and not a direct source port. When the user asks to use owned X26/Anca/Creative Taste Bot code directly, first port the source mechanic 1:1, then adapt the AIM/payment composition around it. X26-derived payment variants should use owned presentation math/code for pointer inertia, depth rotation, strand/bowl/event-horizon motion, and velocity wake. Treat AIM Payment Gravity and AIM Vortex Checkout as 7.5/10 archive candidates only after a stronger replacement is user-approved.

After materially redesigning an already rated artifact, add ratingPolicy.ratingResetAt and ratingPolicy.needsSonyaRereview when it should go back to Anka/Sonya review; do not promote or sort by stale ratings as current approval.

updateRules.title: AIM Night Design Pursuit
inputs: Creative Taste Bot generated animations and research dashboard when available
outputs: V3 Site Repo - aimwebsite0.5/payment-page-design-lab/runs/<timestamp-slug>/ or updated payment lab variants
policy: Owner-approved resource window is 00:00-07:00 Europe/Lisbon.
During that window, the worker may quit nonessential apps such as Telegram, Obsidian, Pencil, Linear, extra Terminal sessions, stale subagents, and stale local servers not needed for validation.
Preserve Codex, needed browser/Arc, OS services, Finder, Yandex Disk/sync services, and active Creative Taste Bot/code-animation automation processes.
The optional local bug continuation automation may stay paused while this night-design lane is active.
Generate diverse review-only designs across homepage, guided payment page, and standalone element lanes.
Do not repeat the same motif; each night should vary structure, interaction, typography, imagery/media system, density, mood, and content emphasis.

Do not generate many variants of the same page shell with only background/accent/ambient changes. At most two same-shell variants may be kept for comparison, and only if the README/manifest states exactly what changed.
AIM Design Quality Pursuit target is 8+/10. If expected score is below 8, iterate or mark as failed exploration.
Treat the latest Sonya generator handoff events as high-priority taste instructions: hard rejects block repetition, weak ratings require mutation, high ratings become style anchors.
Do not create fake proof, fake reviews, archive cards without links, or source-thin complete pages.
Do not commit, push, deploy, delete, rename, or replace production/staging pages.
Exception: Surikat Sonya may publish only review-only design lab artifacts to GitHub Pages after sonyaScore > 5; this does not authorize live route replacement or unrelated staging.
The automation is scheduled repeatedly through the quiet window; do not treat it as a one-shot short burst.
After the 2026-05-28 rejection, do not repeat over-literal AIM OS / beige-grid / monospaced Russian homepage or element directions.

Homepage and element runs must pass screenshot readability gates before being promoted: readable proportional Russian body copy, high contrast, calm text surfaces, no dense grid/constellation noise under paragraphs.

Payment Event Horizon learning: keep the mouse-dependent glow from AIM Payment Gravity, but do not repeat simple vertical galaxy sway. The rejected AIM Event Horizon was a local 2D approximation, not a Gemini-assisted review and not a direct source port. When the user asks to use owned X26/Anca/Creative Taste Bot code directly, first port the source mechanic 1:1, then adapt the AIM/payment composition around it. X26-derived payment variants should use owned presentation math/code for pointer inertia, depth rotation, strand/bowl/event-horizon motion, and velocity wake. Treat AIM Payment Gravity and AIM Vortex Checkout as 7.5/10 archive candidates only after a stronger replacement is user-approved.

After materially redesigning an already rated artifact, add ratingPolicy.ratingResetAt and ratingPolicy.needsSonyaRereview when it should go back to Anka/Sonya review; do not promote or sort by stale ratings as current approval.

updateRules.title: AIM Gemini Page Generation Sprint
updateRules.cadence: one-off manual sprint in the current quiet window
policy: Route is Gemini visual scout -> Codex implementation -> local/browser QA.
Run /Users/viola/All/Yandex.Disk.localized/3 Process/8 Vibe Coding/agent-tools/gemini-web-ui-scout.sh before each open-ended page/UI artifact.

If Gemini fails because of auth, quota, timeout, rate limit, malformed output, or CLI error, run /Users/viola/All/Yandex.Disk.localized/3 Process/8 Vibe Coding/agent-tools/web-ui-model-fallback-scout.sh and continue with a fallback label.
Prioritize Route B from the agent route experiment queue.
Do not shrink the run to one card, one route, or one queued item unless a real blocker is documented.
Prefer full homepage concepts and payment-page concepts first; include elements only when queue or feedback makes that more useful.
Do not use Flow as the default visual scout.
Do not enable Vertex, GenMedia, Veo, Imagen, Lyria, or other billable media generation.
Do not commit, push, deploy, delete, rename, replace live routes, or edit production/staging pages.
Every artifact must include index.html, README.md, Gemini scout path, fallback status if any, source notes, feedback response, self-rating, and validation notes.
Validate JSON and manifest paths; when local preview can run, check desktop/mobile for no horizontal overflow, no blank canvases/iframes, and immediate Russian readability.

updateRules.title: Homepage element design generator

inputs: /Users/viola/All/Yandex.Disk.localized/3 Process/8 Vibe Coding/evalv : Creative Taste Bot/generated-animations/manifest.json when available
policy: Create exactly one focused homepage block, popup, CTA, card system, footer, or comparable element per run.
Rotate coverage across hero, philosophy, ecosystem/products, programs, formats, cases/reviews, team, consulting, space, non-profit, FAQ, footer, CTA, and popups.

Write artifacts only inside V3 Site Repo - aimwebsite0.5/homepage-design-lab.

Run a source-fidelity pass for copied names, links, CTAs, product labels, and footer/legal/archive references before handoff.
Do not commit, push, deploy, or edit production/staging routes.
Exception: Surikat Sonya may publish only review-only design lab artifacts to GitHub Pages after sonyaScore > 5; this does not authorize live route replacement or unrelated staging.
After the 2026-05-28 rejection, do not repeat over-literal AIM OS / beige-grid / monospaced Russian element directions or self-rate them as 8/10.
Element body copy must use readable proportional typography; monospace is limited to short labels, tags, codes, and metadata.
Before handoff, check desktop/mobile screenshots for immediate readability and hide failed explorations from the default gallery.

Payment Event Horizon learning: keep the mouse-dependent glow from AIM Payment Gravity, but do not repeat simple vertical galaxy sway. X26-derived payment variants should use owned presentation math/code for pointer inertia, depth rotation, strand/bowl/event-horizon motion, and velocity wake. Treat AIM Payment Gravity and AIM Vortex Checkout as 7.5/10 archive candidates only after a stronger replacement is user-approved.

After materially redesigning an already rated artifact, add ratingPolicy.ratingResetAt and ratingPolicy.needsSonyaRereview when it should go back to Anka/Sonya review; do not promote or sort by stale ratings as current approval.

updateRules.title: Local Codex bug continuation
updateRules.trigger: Codex app cron automation with prompt `continue this task`
inputs: current repo/files from the last unresolved bug-style thread in the AIM workspace
outputs: continued local review/fix pass in the active workspace
policy: This automation lives on the local Mac outside the git repo.
automation.toml defines schedule, cwd, model, and the generic continuation prompt.
memory.md is the readable run history: what it checked, what it changed, what remains open.
It is an execution helper, not a source of truth for product rules or site content.
Do not push or deploy without explicit review approval and push authorization.

updateRules.title: Tool registry
updateRules.cadence: on every new reusable website review or design tool
updateRules.trigger: node local-preview/sync-tool-registry-summary.mjs or local preview auto-sync on /aim-site-hub/
policy: Register every reusable review or design tool before handoff.
Use kind=review for compare/QA/preview surfaces and kind=design for visual generators, asset-making tools, and design references.
Do not hardcode chat-only tool links without a source path.
Open the tool through the hub route and check mobile overflow when the hub UI changes.

The local preview server runs the generated hub sync scripts automatically before serving /aim-site-hub/, so newly registered tools should appear after refresh even if the explicit sync command was not run first.
